title: rosa canina f. vinealis
intro: Rosa canina f. vinealis, also known as the dwarf dog rose or Rosa canina var. vinealis, is a variant of the dog rose shrub, native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its compact growth habit and thorny stems. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its hips, which are high in vitamins C and E, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. vinealis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. Rosa canina f. vinealis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
